apiVersion.description: Configuration file syntax version. Must be k0sctl.k0sproject.io/v1beta1.

metadata.description: Information that can be used to uniquely identify the object.

limit.description:
Maximum number of hosts to configure concurrently. Equivalent to --concurrency
on the command line. Set to 0 for unlimited.

workerDisruptionPercent.description:
Maximum percentage of worker nodes that may be disrupted simultaneously during
operations such as upgrade. Value must be between 0 and 100. This ensures a
minimum number of workers remain available during rolling operations.

uploads.description:
Maximum number of file uploads to perform concurrently. Equivalent to
--concurrent-uploads on the command line.

ConcurrencyOption.description: ConcurrencyOption controls how many hosts are operated on at once.

enabled.description:
When false, k0sctl skips draining nodes before disruptive operations such as
upgrade or reset. Equivalent to passing --no-drain on the command line.

gracePeriod.description: How long to wait for pods to be evicted from the node before proceeding.

timeout.description: How long to wait for the entire drain operation to complete before timing out.

force.description:
Pass --force to kubectl drain, allowing pods without a replication controller
to be evicted.

ignoreDaemonSets.description:
Pass --ignore-daemonsets to kubectl drain so that DaemonSet-managed pods are
not considered when draining.

deleteEmptyDirData.description:
Pass --delete-emptydir-data to kubectl drain, allowing pods that use emptyDir
volumes (whose data will be lost) to be evicted.

podSelector.description: Label selector passed to kubectl drain to restrict which pods are considered.

skipWaitForDeleteTimeout.description:
If a pod's DeletionTimestamp is older than this duration, skip waiting for it.
Must be greater than 0s to take effect.

DrainOption.description: DrainOption controls the drain behavior for cluster operations.

enabled.description:
When true, k0sctl applies a taint to nodes before service-affecting operations
(upgrade, reset) to signal workloads to evacuate before the node is disrupted.
Can also be enabled at runtime with --evict-taint on the command line.

taint.description: Taint to apply when enabled is true. Must be in the format key=value.

effect.description: Effect of the taint. Must be NoExecute, NoSchedule, or PreferNoSchedule.

controllerWorkers.description:
When true, the taint is also applied to controller+worker nodes. By default
only pure worker nodes are tainted.

EvictTaintOption.description: EvictTaintOption controls whether and how a taint is applied to nodes before service-affecting operations like upgrade or reset.

address.description: IP address or hostname of the host.

user.description: WinRM user name. The user must have administrative privileges.

port.description: TCP port for the WinRM endpoint. When useHTTPS is true, the default port automatically switches to 5986.

password.description: Password for the WinRM user. Required unless certificate-based authentication is configured.

useHTTPS.description: Enable HTTPS for WinRM. When enabled, set caCertPath (and optionally certPath/keyPath) to verify the remote endpoint.

insecure.description: Skip TLS certificate verification when connecting over HTTPS.

useNTLM.description: Use NTLM authentication instead of basic authentication.

caCertPath.description: Path to a CA bundle used to validate the WinRM server certificate.

certPath.description: Client certificate for mutual TLS authentication.

tlsServerName.description: Override the TLS server name used during certificate verification.

address.description: IP address or hostname of the bastion host.

user.description: SSH user for the bastion host.

port.description: SSH port on the bastion host.

keyPath.description: Path to an SSH private key for the bastion host.

address.description: IP address or hostname of the host.

port.description: TCP port of the SSH service on the host.

keyPath.description: Path to an SSH private key file. If a public key is used, ssh-agent is required. When left empty, the default value will first be looked for from the SSH configuration IdentityFile parameter.

address.description: IP address or hostname of the bastion host.

user.description: SSH user for the bastion host.

port.description: SSH port on the bastion host.

keyPath.description: Path to an SSH private key for the bastion host.

enabled.description: Must be set to true to enable the localhost connection.

address.description: IP address, hostname, or ssh config host alias of the host.

keyPath.description: Path to an SSH private key.

configPath.description: Path to ssh config. Defaults to ~/.ssh/config with fallback to /etc/ssh/ssh_config.

options.description: Additional options as key/value pairs passed to the ssh client as -o flags.

disableMultiplexing.description: Disable SSH connection multiplexing. When true, every remote command requires reconnecting to the host.

role.description:
Role of the host in the cluster. One of:
  - controller — a controller-only node
  - controller+worker — a controller that also runs workloads
  - single — a single-node cluster; the configuration may only contain one host with this role
  - worker — a worker node

reset.description: When true, k0sctl will remove the node from Kubernetes and reset k0s on the host.

privateInterface.description:
Override the private network interface selected by host fact gathering. Useful when
fact gathering picks the wrong interface for intra-cluster communication.

privateAddress.description:
Override the private IP address selected by host fact gathering. Useful when
fact gathering picks the wrong address for intra-cluster communication.

dataDir.description: Override the k0s data directory on the host.

kubeletRootDir.description: Override the kubelet root directory on the host.

environment.description: Environment variables to set in the k0s service environment on the host.

uploadBinary.description:
When true, the k0s binary is downloaded on the local machine and uploaded to the
target host. When false (the default), the binary is downloaded directly on the host.

useExistingK0s.description:
When true, k0sctl reuses the k0s binary that already exists on the host without
downloading or uploading anything. Upgrades for this host are skipped.
Cannot be combined with uploadBinary, k0sBinaryPath, or k0sDownloadURL.

k0sBinaryPath.description:
Path to a local k0s binary to upload to the host. Useful for testing a custom
or development build of k0s without publishing a release.

k0sInstallPath.description: Path on the host where the k0s binary will be installed.

k0sDownloadURL.description:
URL to download the k0s binary from instead of the default k0s GitHub releases.
Supports %-prefixed tokens: %v (version), %p (arch), %x (.exe on Windows).

installFlags.description:
Extra flags passed verbatim to the k0s install command on the host.
See k0s install --help for available options.

files.description:
Files to upload to the host before k0s is configured. Supports local paths,
URLs, and glob patterns. See the file upload documentation for details.

os.description:
Override OS distribution auto-detection. By default k0sctl reads /etc/os-release.
Set this when the release file does not reflect the true distribution, e.g. set
"debian" for a Debian-based image that reports a different OS ID.

hostname.description: Override the hostname reported by the OS. When not set, the OS hostname is used.

noTaints.description:
When true and used with the controller+worker role, disables the default
node-role.kubernetes.io/master:NoSchedule taint so that regular workloads
can be scheduled on the node without requiring a toleration.

before.description: Commands to run before the action.

after.description: Commands to run after the action.

before.description: Commands to run before the action.

after.description: Commands to run after the action.

before.description: Commands to run before the action.

after.description: Commands to run after the action.

before.description: Commands to run before the action.

after.description: Commands to run after the action.

before.description: Commands to run before the action.

after.description: Commands to run after the action.

before.description: Commands to run before the action.

after.description: Commands to run after the action.

hooks.description:
Commands to run on the host at specific points during k0sctl operations.
See the hooks documentation for available stages and timing details.

Host.description: Host contains all the needed details to work with hosts

version.description:
Version of k0s to deploy. When omitted, k0sctl selects the latest stable release
(or the version already running on the cluster if one exists).

versionChannel.description:
Version channel used when auto-discovering the k0s version. Set to "latest" to
allow k0sctl to select pre-release versions. Has no effect when version is set.

dynamicConfig.description:
Enable k0s dynamic configuration. When true, k0sctl only pushes the cluster-wide
configuration on first-time initialisation; subsequent applies leave it unchanged.
Use k0sctl config edit or k0s config edit to manage it afterwards.
This flag is also auto-enabled when any controller has --enable-dynamic-config in
installFlags or in its running k0s arguments.

config.description:
Embedded k0s cluster configuration. See https://docs.k0sproject.io/stable/configuration/
for field reference. When omitted, the output of k0s config create is used.
The k0s config can also be placed as a separate YAML document in the same file,
using apiVersion: k0s.k0sproject.io/v1beta1 and kind: ClusterConfig.

K0s.description: K0s holds configuration for bootstraping a k0s cluster

wait.description: Controls wait behavior for cluster operations.

drain.description: Controls drain behavior for cluster operations.

concurrency.description: Controls how many hosts are operated on at once.

evictTaint.description: Controls whether a taint is applied to nodes before disruptive operations.

k0s.description: Settings related to the k0s cluster.

Spec.description: Spec defines cluster config spec section

name.description: Optional label for this upload entry, used only in log output.

src.description:
Source file path, URL, or glob pattern. Required when data is not set.
Glob patterns follow the doublestar syntax. URL sources are downloaded directly
on the target host. Supports %v, %p, %x token expansion.

data.description: Inline file content to write to the destination. Required when src is not set.

dstDir.description:
Destination directory on the host. k0sctl creates the full path if it does
not exist. Defaults to the remote user's home directory.

dst.description:
Destination filename on the host. Only valid for single-file uploads.
Defaults to the source file's basename.

perm.description:
Permission mode for the uploaded file(s), e.g. 0644. Defaults to the local
file's permission mode.

dirPerm.description: Permission mode for directories created by k0sctl during upload.

user.description:
Owner user name for the uploaded file(s) and created directories. Must already
exist on the host.

group.description:
Owner group name for the uploaded file(s) and created directories. Must already
exist on the host.

UploadFile.description: UploadFile describes a file to be uploaded for the host

enabled.description:
When false, k0sctl will not wait for k0s to become ready after restarting the
service. Equivalent to passing --no-wait on the command line.

WaitOption.description: WaitOption controls the wait behavior for cluster operations.

title: k0sctl configuration
description: Configuration file for k0sctl - a bootstrapping and management tool for k0s clusters.
